preview_text:
The model favors Canada with a 41% win probability, against Switzerland's 28%. The statistical forecast ranks Canada as the clearer favorite, though the underdog retains a meaningful chance to prevail at neutral venue conditions.

The 31% draw probability signals a competitive match. The gap between Canada's modeled win rate and Switzerland's is substantial—roughly 13 percentage points—but the elevated draw odds suggest the teams are not drastically apart in strength. A one-goal outcome leans toward the model's assessment, though a stalemate remains plausible.

Model and sportsbook consensus diverge sharply. The market backs Switzerland to win at 45%, a gap of 17 percentage points above the model's 28% estimate. Polymarket prices align closely with the book consensus, placing Switzerland slightly higher still. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence reflects genuine disagreement on relative team strength.
